questions.question: Which of the following ancient texts is primarily associated with the early Vedic period?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda is one of the oldest texts associated with the early Vedic period in India.

questions.question: Who was the founder of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in India in 1526 after the Battle of Panipat.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Salt Satyagraha in 1930?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Salt Satyagraha, a major nonviolent protest against British salt laws.

questions.question: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in 1920 as a mass protest against British rule.

questions.question: Who was the first President of Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee was the first President of the Indian National Congress in 1885.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganges is the longest river in India, flowing approximately 2,525 km.

questions.question: The monsoon season in India is primarily caused by:
questions.explanation: The southwest monsoon winds bring heavy rainfall to India between June and September.

questions.question: Which state is the largest producer of coal in India?
questions.explanation: Chhattisgarh is the largest coal-producing state in India.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the highest to India’s GDP?
questions.explanation: The services sector contributes the highest share to India’s GDP.

questions.question: Who is the chief architect of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar is known as the chief architect of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: The Lok Sabha is constituted for a term of:
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha has a tenure of 5 years unless dissolved sooner.

questions.question: The Supreme Court of India was established in which year?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of India was established in 1950.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992, is related to:
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act relates to Rural Local Bodies (Panchayats).

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right provides freedom of speech and expression?
questions.explanation: The Right to Freedom guarantees freedom of speech and expression.

questions.question: The introduction of Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India was implemented in which year?
questions.explanation: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017.

questions.question: Which organization publishes the 'Financial Stability Report' in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) publishes the Financial Stability Report.

questions.category: Indian Economy - Five Year Plans & Trade
questions.question: India’s first Five Year Plan was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: India’s first Five Year Plan began in 1951.

questions.question: Which of the following is a noble gas?
questions.explanation: Helium is a noble gas and inert in nature.

questions.question: The element responsible for carrying oxygen in blood is:
questions.explanation: Iron in hemoglobin binds oxygen to carry it in blood.

questions.question: Which India’s space mission was the first to reach Mars orbit?
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan, launched in 2013, was India’s first interplanetary mission reaching Mars orbit.

questions.question: Which of the following is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in India?
questions.explanation: All listed sites are UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India.

questions.question: The Kyoto Protocol is related to:
questions.explanation: Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty for addressing climate change.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest national park in India?
options: Gulf of Mannar Marine National Park
questions.explanation: Kaziranga National Park is the largest in India, known for its one-horned rhinoceros.

questions.question: The Biological Diversity Act, 2002, aims to:
questions.explanation: The act covers conservation, sustainable use, and fair sharing of benefits from bioprospecting.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu is the current President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi has been the Prime Minister since 26 May 2014.

questions.question: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar is the current External Affairs Minister of India.

questions.question: Which is the fastest land animal in India?
questions.explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land animal, capable of speeds up to 100 km/h.

questions.question: Which legislation aims to protect the ozone layer in India?
questions.explanation: India’s Ozone Cell under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change oversees ozone protection laws.
